seed_problem: Build a connected relay network where no node has degree > 3, minimizing total cable length; checker validates connectivity + degree and scores baseline/cost.
mutation: output+input: keep the min-cost connectivity goal but (a) replace the uniform degree-<=3 bound with per-station heterogeneous port limits d_v in {2,3}, and (b) restrict cabling to an arbitrary catalogue of candidate edges (not a complete geometric graph), so the optimal connected spanning subgraph is a degree-constrained bounded spanning tree = NP-hard, and degree-aware greedy vs. edge-swap local search diverge.
title: Arena Relay Fabric: Port-Bounded Cable Layout
strategies: daisy-chain baseline: install the cheapest cable between each consecutive station pair (always feasible, degree <= 2)
degree-aware Kruskal: add candidate cables cheapest-first, skipping any that form a cycle or would exceed a station's port limit, then repair to full connectivity
edge-swap local search: start from the greedy fabric and repeatedly cut an installed cable and reconnect the two sides with the cheapest feasible cross-cut cable that respects port limits
randomized multi-restart local search over the swap neighbourhood, keeping the cheapest feasible fabric found
